name: jsonwebtoken v8.5.1 — real CVEs fixed in v9.0.0
context: JWT signing and verification library. v8.5.1 has 4 CVEs fixed in v9.0.0. Focus on security bugs in verify.js and sign.js.

real_bugs.description: When token has no signature and no algorithms option is provided, verify() silently defaults to algorithms=['none'], accepting unsigned tokens. This allows signature bypass — any attacker can forge an unsigned token and it will be accepted unless the caller explicitly sets algorithms.
real_bugs.fix_in_v9: Changed to require explicit 'none' in algorithms option, returns error otherwise

real_bugs.description: Algorithm selection when options.algorithms is not set uses string matching on the key (indexOf('BEGIN CERTIFICATE'), indexOf('BEGIN PUBLIC KEY')) to guess allowed algorithms. This enables algorithm confusion attacks: an attacker can specify alg:HS256 in the JWT header, and if the server's RSA public key string doesn't match the patterns, it falls through to HS_ALGS, using the public key as HMAC secret — allowing token forgery.
real_bugs.fix_in_v9: Uses crypto.KeyObject type detection instead of string matching, with proper key type enforcement

real_bugs.description: No minimum key size enforcement. RSA keys smaller than 2048 bits are accepted for RS256/RS384/RS512/PS256/PS384/PS512, making signatures trivially breakable with modern hardware.
real_bugs.fix_in_v9: Added minimum 2048-bit key size check for RSA/PS algorithms, with allowInsecureKeySizes opt-out

real_bugs.description: No validation that key type matches algorithm. HMAC secrets can be used with asymmetric algorithms and vice versa. sign() with algorithm RS256 and a string secret will silently produce a broken token. verify() similarly has no key-algorithm type check.
real_bugs.fix_in_v9: Added KeyObject conversion, key type checks (HS requires secret, RS/PS/ES require private/public), and validateAsymmetricKey()

not_bugs.description: Uses var instead of const/let throughout. Style preference, not a bug. Changed to const/let in v9 as part of modernization.
Imports individual lodash packages (lodash.includes, lodash.isboolean, etc). Dependency hygiene concern, not a security bug.
Uses ~options.algorithms.indexOf() bitwise trick. Unusual but functionally correct.
decode is exported as enumerable property. Changed in v9 to non-enumerable to discourage direct use without verify, but not a security bug.

debatable.description: secretOrPublicKey is used directly without type coercion. If passed an object with toString(), it could produce unexpected results in the string matching algorithm selection. Borderline prototype pollution vector.
options.clockTimestamp || Math.floor(Date.now()/1000) means clockTimestamp=0 is treated as falsy and replaced with current time. Edge case, unlikely in practice.

PUB_KEY_ALGS includes both RSA and EC algorithms in one array. When key string matches 'BEGIN CERTIFICATE', all of these are allowed. This means an EC key could be attempted with RS256. Related to algorithm confusion but more nuanced.
